concept: Recognize / Identify / See and Know Again
description: To perceive something or someone and connect that perception to prior knowledge or understanding. 'Miratu' bridges present perception with memory—the moment when what is before us meets what we already know, creating identification and connection.
sound_symbolism: The word moves from inner to outer knowing. 'Mi' begins with nasal inwardness, the self perceiving. Then 'ra' reaches outward with rolling awareness, perception extending. Finally 'tu' arrives with dental precision, identification confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia thia miratu.' (I recognize you.) Can specify what is recognized: 'mia welao thena miratu.' (I recognize a good thing.) Distinct from shelomu (understand)—miratu is identification, shelomu is comprehension.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Recognition is mindful perception meeting memory—seeing clearly what is present while connecting it to accumulated understanding.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Recognizing others affirms their existence and continuity—I see you as someone with history and identity.
